description_long: This sculpture depicts the Hindu deity Ganesha, characterized by an elephant head and a rounded human body, seated in a traditional pose. The figure is richly detailed, with visible trunk, tusks, large ears, and ornate headgear, as well as jewelry and decorative elements on the body. The sculpture is rendered in two distinct color schemes: one side is a reddish-brown with gold highlights, while the other is a greenish-blue with darker patina, suggesting either different materials or weathering effects. The base is circular and features carved patterns, providing stability and a sense of completeness. The surface texture appears rough and weathered, with visible tool marks and irregularities, enhancing its handcrafted appearance. The object is solid and compact, likely intended for display in a public or semi-public urban space.
description: A detailed, dual-toned sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, featuring ornate carvings and a weathered, rough surface. The figure is seated with prominent elephant features and decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the reddish-brown and greenish-blue sides of the Ganesha figure, highlighting the detailed facial features, trunk, and ornate headgear.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the greenish-blue patina, emphasizing the profile of the elephant head and the intricate folds of the seated posture.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded back and the circular base, with both color schemes visible and the back of the headgear and body details.
description_view_3: Side view dominated by the reddish-brown coloration, showcasing the side profile of the face, trunk, and decorative elements on the body.
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the object could be placed in an urban park, plaza, or temple setting.
